name: pdfrw (Pure Python PDF Read/Write)

performance.notes: Pure Python; fast for structural manipulation, no rendering

agent_readiness.notes: Pure Python PDF manipulation — merge, split, overlay, fill forms. from pdfrw import PdfReader, PdfWriter. trailer = PdfReader('input.pdf'). writer = PdfWriter(). writer.addpage(trailer.pages[0]). writer.write('output.pdf'). Merge PDFs: writer.addpages(reader.pages). Overlay/watermark: template + page overlay. Form filling: PdfDict objects. Primarily structural — does not render or extract text. pikepdf and pypdf are better-maintained alternatives. Development stalled since 2018.

gotchas: Development stalled since 2018 — pypdf and pikepdf are more maintained
Structural manipulation only — no text extraction, no rendering
Documentation is sparse — mostly README and examples
Complex PDF features (encryption, signatures) not well supported
pypdf (formerly PyPDF2) is a better-maintained pure-Python alternative
best_when: Simple PDF merging, splitting, and page reordering
When pypdf isn't suitable and you need pure Python
avoid_when: New projects — use pypdf or pikepdf instead
Complex PDF manipulation with encryption or digital signatures
summary: pdfrw manipulates PDFs structurally in pure Python. from pdfrw import PdfReader, PdfWriter; reader = PdfReader('in.pdf'); writer = PdfWriter(); writer.addpages(reader.pages); writer.write('out.pdf'). MIT. Stalled since 2018 — prefer pypdf or pikepdf for new projects. Good for simple merge/split/reorder. No text extraction or rendering.
